title: Red eye documentation

presenting_history.text: Describes unilateral or bilateral redness, watery, mucus or purulent discharge, crusting, itching, burning, grittiness, foreign-body sensation, contact spread, dry-eye symptoms, and mechanical or chemical insult context.
presenting_history.rationale: Supports redness, discharge, itch, burning, grittiness and exposure features for red-eye documentation; no decision is generated.
presenting_history.evidence_extract: Describes unilateral or bilateral redness, watery, mucus or purulent discharge, crusting, itching, burning, grittiness, foreign-body sensation, contact spread, dry-eye symptoms, and mechanical or chemical insult context.

source.title: DHA Telehealth Clinical Guidelines for Virtual Management of Conjunctivitis

exact_section.heading: Sections 5.1–5.9 — infectious, allergic, and non-infectious features

red_flags.text: Lists reduced visual acuity, ciliary flush, photophobia, severe foreign-body sensation, corneal opacity, fixed pupil, severe headache with nausea, post-traumatic red eye, and immune compromise.
red_flags.rationale: Supports reduced acuity, photophobia, severe pain, corneal, pupil and trauma warnings for red-eye documentation; no decision is generated.
red_flags.evidence_extract: Lists reduced visual acuity, ciliary flush, photophobia, severe foreign-body sensation, corneal opacity, fixed pupil, severe headache with nausea, post-traumatic red eye, and immune compromise.

source.title: DHA Telehealth Clinical Guidelines for Virtual Management of Conjunctivitis

examination.text: Requires history and visual review for red eye and discharge, distinguishes discharge character, asks about vision, foreign-body sensation, photophobia, trauma and contact lenses, and highlights keratitis risk in contact-lens wearers.
examination.rationale: Supports red-eye history and examination documentation for red-eye documentation; no decision is generated.
examination.evidence_extract: Requires history and visual review for red eye and discharge, distinguishes discharge character, asks about vision, foreign-body sensation, photophobia, trauma and contact lenses, and highlights keratitis risk in contact-lens wearers.

exact_section.heading: Section 7 — diagnosis, history, pitfalls, and contact-lens wearers

source_limitations: All item-level mappings remain unsupported pending separate clinician review and signed approval.
Eye, onset, pain, discharge, itch, vision, photophobia, trauma, contact lenses, examination, diagnosis and clinician disposition are absent.
No diagnosis, interpretation, investigation, treatment, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, escalation or disposition is generated for red-eye documentation.
Any legacy item not explicitly mapped to an exact section remains unsupported pending qualified clinician review.
No mapping authorises an automatic diagnosis, differential, investigation, management decision, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, urgency conclusion, discharge decision, or patient instruction.
curation_policy: Direct guideline curation: retain only explicitly mapped content; rewrite only with explicit item mapping; remove unmapped legacy content; add only committed exact-section evidence summaries.
